candidates.direction_name: Deepen The Active PTX Hot-Band To A 3-Stage Pg2s Pipeline
ranking_notes: The current run already cleaned up barrier, so the remaining visible gap is the latency-hiding pair long_scoreboard plus mio_throttle.
This follows the Async Copy, Pg2s, and Stage guidance directly without replaying the exact register-interleave fingerprint that already failed.
It keeps the active PTX dispatch and only deepens overlap; if it loses, the result will still teach whether the current surface is pipeline-limited or occupancy-limited.
candidates.hypothesis: After restoring launch_bounds to 2, the PTX anchor regained low barrier and low short-scoreboard, but the remaining gap is now long_scoreboard plus mio_throttle at only 16.60% active warps. A 3-stage Pg2s ring should hide more global-to-shared latency by keeping one extra refill in flight, so the kernel can recover tensor activity without needing the failed register-interleave rewrite.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Global-to-shared latency hiding at low occupancy: long_scoreboard 5.45% and mio_throttle 3.98% indicate cp.async completion is still arriving too late for the current two-stage PTX pipeline.
candidates.risk: Moderate-high. This touches the PTX prolog, the steady-state refill order, and the drain sequencing together, and it can easily grow shared memory or destabilize wait_group ordering if done sloppily.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the edit forces shared memory materially above the current PTX budget or if wait_group sequencing requires a broader launch-family rewrite.
expected_local_changes: Expand the PTX a_shared and b_shared ring from two stages to three.
Retune the PTX prolog and steady-state wait_group ordering to keep an extra refill in flight.
Leave the active PTX dispatch and grouped_rows constant unchanged in the first pipeline-depth probe.

candidates.direction_name: Split The Final PTX Wait/Sync Drain Out Of The Steady-State Loop
ranking_notes: This remains the smallest open PTX-only cleanup on the current accepted base surface.
It is now rank 2 instead of rank 1 because barrier is no longer the dominant loss; long_scoreboard and mio_throttle are larger remaining problems.
If the 3-stage pipeline is too risky or blocked, this is the next cheapest way to probe further synchronization cleanup.
candidates.hypothesis: Even after the launch-bounds rollback, the PTX loop still pays cp_async_wait_group_0 plus __syncthreads whenever next_tile_idx < FixedKTiles, including the penultimate tile where future_tile_idx is already out of range and no refill follows. Pulling that no-refill drain into a tiny epilogue should shave the remaining barrier seam without changing the current register footprint or stage depth.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Residual barrier tax from the final no-refill handoff inside the PTX steady-state loop.
candidates.risk: Moderate. The change is local and keeps the same PTX dispatch, but it modifies a performance-sensitive loop boundary and can break correctness if the final drain is mistimed.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if splitting the drain requires another shared-memory stage, wider launch changes, or extra steady-state predicates beyond a tiny epilogue case.
expected_local_changes: Split the no-refill drain from the current PTX steady-state wait/sync block.
Keep stage count at 2 and leave the current PTX launch surface unchanged.
Do not touch grouped_rows or the 64x64 row-pair accumulator path in the first pass.

candidates.direction_name: Reopen 256x128 64x64-Warp Hot-Band Tiling On The Dominant Surface
ranking_notes: This keeps the explicit 256x128 / 64x64 human guidance alive without pretending it is the first thing to do on a surface that just recovered locally.
It is still the highest-ceiling structural branch left if the local PTX latency-hiding directions plateau again.
Rank 3 is deliberate: the current PTX path is close enough to its accepted baseline that broader tiling work should wait until the local families are squeezed.
candidates.hypothesis: The compact PTX 128x128 path is now back near its accepted baseline, but it is still almost 0.9 ms off the best known custom result and still capped at 16.60% active warps. The source already carries a 256x128 hot-band surface, and the persistent human guidance still asks for 256x128 block tiling with 64x64 warp tiling plus Right-Left-Right-Left reuse. Reopening that family is the next structural branch once the local PTX surface has been deconfounded.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band tiling and warp-level reuse ceiling: the 128x128 PTX surface may simply have run out of occupancy and locality headroom.
candidates.risk: High. This is a broader geometry reopen with known correctness and performance history, and it touches writer ownership, export layout, and launch routing together.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness on all 3 dataset cases before trusting runtime
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the reopen requires a broad writer-ownership replay or reintroduces previously failed export layouts instead of a writer-safe 256x128 path.
expected_local_changes: Reopen the fixed hot-band launch routing for the 256x128 dominant surface.
Keep writer ownership explicit and export-safe.
Preserve the 64x64 warp-tile reuse pattern rather than reviving the broken half-panel writer split.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
